description: The passenger details form allows submission when the phone number is left empty. The user can click 'Continue to seat selection' with only name and email filled; the form proceeds to the next step without showing a validation error for the missing phone.
expectedOutcome: Phone is required for contact. Submitting the form with an empty phone field should show a 'Phone is required' validation error and keep the user on the passenger details page; navigation to seat selection should not occur.
hint: The passenger form should require phone for contact; check which field has no validation rules.
symptom: Form submits and navigates to seat selection even when phone is left empty.
solution: Add required validation to the phone field in PassengerFields and display its validation error (label 'Phone *', register with { required: 'Phone is required' }, and render the error span with role='alert' and aria-invalid on the input).
exactCorrectFix: Label: Phone *; register: {...register(`passengers.${i}.phone`, { required: 'Phone is required' })}; add aria-invalid on input; add error span: {errors?.passengers?.[i]?.phone && ({errors.passengers[i].phone.message})}
solutionCode: In PassengerFields: (1) Change the phone label to 'Phone *'. (2) Replace {...register(`passengers.${i}.phone`)} with {...register(`passengers.${i}.phone`, { required: 'Phone is required' })} and set aria-invalid={errors?.passengers?.[i]?.phone ? 'true' : 'false'} on the input. (3) After the phone input, add the error display: {errors?.passengers?.[i]?.phone && ({errors.passengers[i].phone.message})}
playwrightTestSnippets: test('submitting passenger form with empty phone shows validation error and stays on passengers page', async ({ page }) => { ... fill name and email, leave phone empty, submit ... await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/passengers/); await expect(page.getByRole('alert').filter({ hasText: /phone is required/i })).toBeVisible(); });
